MWCapture SDK  3.3.1.1556
  • Home
  • Development Library
  • Examples
  • Release Notes
MultiStreaming C++

The features are as follows:

using the interface of group-functions-procapture to capture and preview three video streams separately from the same input video channel. Note that this example opens the capture device three times simultaneously, so only Pro Capture Family is supported.

Folder dir: SDKv3\Examples\VC++\GUI\MultiStreaming

Supported Hardware

Pro Capture Family

Features

  1. Preview captured videos.
  2. Capture and preview the selected channel 3 times simultaneously, the application interface is shown in the "Result” part.
  3. Switch capture channel mid-stream, without exiting the application.

Workflow

The main thread
  1. Capture 3 video streams and each stream is processed as the workflow of the main thread shows as follows.
  2. To initialize and enumerate all available devices, call: MWCaptureInitInstance, MWGetChannelCount
  3. To find the valid capture card according to the device info,, call: MWGetChannelInfoByIndex
  4. To open the default channel, call: MWGetDevicePath, MWOpenChannelByPath
  5. To get the status of input video, call: MWGetVideoSignalStatus
  6. To create a video renderer, call: MWDXRender
  7. Create a video process thread.
  8. Enter the main thread.
  9. Close and re-open current video when switching to another channel.
  10. To stop recording, andfree up system resources and memory, after exiting the application, call: MWCloseChannel and MWCaptureExitInstance
The video process thread:
  1. To start video capture and create a pool of buffers, call: MWStartVideoCapture, MWPinVideoBuffer
  2. To register a timer, call: MWRegisterTimer
  3. To capture a frame of video data triggered by the scheduled timer, call: MWScheduleTimer, MWCaptureVideoFrameToVirtualAddressEx
  4. To obtain current capture status, and free the memory of the capture card, call: MWGetVideoCaptureStatus
  5. Render captured videos.
  6. Repeat step 3 to 5, until the thread quits.
  7. To exit this thread, and release the resources, call: MWUnpinVideoBuffer, MWStopVideoCapture

Flow Chart

MultiStreaming

Result

MultiStreaming
